name: Cabibbo-angle / first-row CKM unitarity anomaly
summary: The first-row CKM unitarity test |V_ud|^2 + |V_us|^2 + |V_ub|^2 = 1 currently shows a ~3 sigma deficit when V_ud is extracted from superallowed nuclear beta decays and V_us from kaon semileptonic decays. The tension was sharpened post-2018 by improvements in nuclear inner radiative corrections (Seng-Gorchtein-Patel-Ramsey-Musolf 2018, arXiv:1807.10197). Either there is undiscovered new physics in beta decays (lepton-universality violation, right-handed currents) or systematic effects in V_ud / V_us extraction remain.

characteristic_numbers.note: Roughly 3-3.5 sigma deficit using superallowed V_ud and K_l3 V_us. Treatment of nuclear corrections (Seng et al. radiative corrections, structure-dependent) determines significance.

principles_stressed.note: If real, points to ~10^-3 - 10^-4 corrections to charged-current weak interaction, possibly from vector-like quarks, W' bosons, or right-handed currents. New-physics scales >~ 10 TeV typically needed.

history: Long history of <2 sigma tensions; sharpened post-2018 with improved nuclear corrections (Seng-Gorchtein-Patel-Ramsey-Musolf, arXiv:1807.10197). Crivellin-Hoferichter program (arXiv:2002.07184 onward) treats it systematically as a global SMEFT-fit anomaly. Crivellin-Hoferichter 'Explaining the Cabibbo angle anomaly' (arXiv:2111.04519) and SMEFT global fits (Cirigliano-Crivellin-Hoferichter-Moulson 2022, arXiv:2208.11707) constrain new physics.

references.citation: Seng, Gorchtein, Patel, Ramsey-Musolf, 'Reduced hadronic uncertainty in the determination of V_ud', Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 241804 (2018)
Crivellin and Hoferichter, 'Beta decays as sensitive probes of lepton flavor universality', Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 111801 (2020)
